title: papaver rhoeas var. cruciatum
intro: Papaver rhoeas var. cruciatum, also known as the red poppy or Shirley poppy, is a variety of the red poppy plant that is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its alkaloid compounds, such as morphine and thebaine. The health benefits of Papaver rhoeas var. cruciatum include relief from pain, inflammation, and respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
